meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Build Faster & Scale
content.seo_description: Stop guessing your SaaS pricing. Learn how to overcome no SaaS knowledge with this expert guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship profitable apps.
content.answer_block: SaaS pricing isn't about guesswork; it's about value-based tiers. By leveraging Antigravity to scaffold your billing logic and Claude Code to automate implementation, you can ship professional, subscription-ready SaaS apps in hours. Focus on simple 'Starter' and 'Pro' tiers to maximize conversion while minimizing technical overhead for your clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, a client deadline looming, and the crushing weight of a SaaS project you aren't sure how to price. The tech is the easy part, but the business logic—the invisible engine that turns code into recurring revenue—feels like a black box. You’ve built websites, but you haven't built a business.

Today, the dynamic shifts. We are stripping away the complexity of SaaS architecture. Using the precision of Antigravity and the agentic power of Claude Code, we’ll move from 'freelancer building features' to 'architect building revenue streams.' It’s time to stop writing code that doesn't pay and start shipping systems that scale.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing a line of code, establish your pricing logic. We focus on two tiers to keep your implementation lean and your client's roadmap clear.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing model with two tiers: 'Starter' ($29/mo) and 'Pro' ($99/mo). Include fields for feature flags and Stripe product IDs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a TypeScript interface for the PricingTier schema defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Automate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to a real payment processor without manual boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Stripe checkout session handler in Next.js that accepts a priceId and creates a subscription session.

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Restrict access to premium features based on the user's subscription status retrieved from the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks if a user has the 'Pro' flag in the database before allowing access to the /dashboard/analytics route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit middleware.ts --apply 'Implement auth-based route protection for premium features'

steps.title: Build Automated Billing Webhooks
steps.description: Ensure your app stays in sync with Stripe events like 'invoice.payment_succeeded' or 'customer.subscription.deleted'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust webhook handler for Stripe events that updates the user's subscription status in a Supabase/PostgreSQL database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a webhook endpoint that validates Stripe signatures and updates user records'

steps.title: Deploy and Test Flow
steps.description: Validate the end-to-end flow from pricing page selection to database update.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite using Playwright to simulate a user upgrading from Starter to Pro and verifying feature access.

pro_tips: Always use 'Trial' periods to lower the barrier to entry; add a 7-day free trial flag in your Antigravity schema.
Never hardcode prices; store them in your database so you can adjust them without redeploying code.
Implement 'Usage-based' tracking early, even if you start with flat monthly fees—it’s the easiest way to upsell later.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your pricing logic; edge cases like failed payments are where you lose revenue.
Send automated 'Dunning' emails when a subscription fails; it saves 15-20% of your MRR automatically.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on building an 'Admin Dashboard' for your client to track MRR in real-time.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn any SaaS idea into a profitable reality. Don't let the complexity of business logic hold you back from your next big project. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
